explicit_text: I was born in 1892 and remember the Russo-Japanese War well. During that time, we dressed as nurses and participated in a program to support the soldiers returning to our town. Everyone gathered, including various generals, and I recall General Nogi Maresuke, who tragically lost his two sons in the war and later committed suicide with his wife following Emperor Meiji's death.
implicit_text: I was born in 1892 and remember the conflict between the Russian Empire and Japan well. During that time, we dressed as nurses and participated in a program to support the soldiers returning to our town. Everyone gathered, including various generals, and I recall a general who tragically lost his two sons in that war and later committed suicide with his wife following the death of the emperor.

explicit_text: I remember World War I, though I was quite young at the time and only knew that it happened. It was one of the many wars I lived through, along with the Russo-Japanese War and the Manchukuo War. My memories of it are limited, but it was part of the turbulent times in my early life.
implicit_text: I remember the global conflict that took place from 1914 to 1918, though I was quite young at the time and only knew that it happened. It was one of the many wars I lived through, along with the earlier war between Russia and Japan and the later conflict in Manchuria. My memories of it are limited, but it was part of the turbulent times in my early life.

explicit_text: During the Manchukuo War, I remember many tragedies occurring. At Nikoryo, a place in Manchukuo, the Japanese were massacred, which left a deep impression on me and my family. My mother looked at stereoscope pictures of the massacre scenes and cried, fearing what might have happened if her child had been there.
implicit_text: During the conflict in the puppet state established in northeastern China, I remember many tragedies occurring. At a place called Nikoryo within that region, many Japanese were massacred, which left a deep impression on me and my family. My mother looked at stereoscope pictures of the massacre scenes and cried, fearing what might have happened if her child had been there.

explicit_text: General Nogi Maresuke was a figure I remember from the Russo-Japanese War. He lost both of his sons in that war, and after Emperor Meiji died, he and his wife committed suicide to follow the emperor. This story was well known in my town and left a strong impression on us all.
implicit_text: There was a general I remember from the war between Russia and Japan. He lost both of his sons in that war, and after the emperor died, he and his wife committed suicide to follow the emperor. This story was well known in my town and left a strong impression on us all.

explicit_text: Emperor Meiji's death was a significant event during my youth. It was closely linked to the tragic story of General Nogi Maresuke, who, after losing his sons in the Russo-Japanese War, committed suicide with his wife to follow the emperor. The emperor's passing marked the end of an era and deeply affected many people in my town.
implicit_text: The death of the emperor who reigned during the Meiji era was a significant event during my youth. It was closely linked to the tragic story of the general who, after losing his sons in the war between Russia and Japan, committed suicide with his wife to follow the emperor. The emperor's passing marked the end of an era and deeply affected many people in my town.

explicit_text: Nikoryo was a place I remember from the Manchukuo War, where many Japanese were massacred. My mother showed me stereoscope pictures of the massacre scenes there, which made her cry and worry deeply about the safety of children like me. It was a tragic and fearful memory from those times.
implicit_text: Nikoryo was a place I remember from the conflict in the northeastern Chinese puppet state, where many Japanese were massacred. My mother showed me stereoscope pictures of the massacre scenes there, which made her cry and worry deeply about the safety of children like me. It was a tragic and fearful memory from those times.

explicit_text: I was born near Kumamoto, about four ri east of Kumamoto City, in Kamimashiki gun. My home was peaceful, and I attended school there, walking long distances to junior high. Kumamoto City also had a Christian church, which I learned about when I was hospitalized and visited by a Christian minister.
implicit_text: I was born near a city in Kyushu, about four ri east of the city center, in Kamimashiki district. My home was peaceful, and I attended school there, walking long distances to junior high. That city also had a Christian church, which I learned about when I was hospitalized and visited by a Christian minister.

explicit_text: My family's home religion was Buddhist, specifically Shinshū. This was the faith I was raised in, and it shaped much of my early life. Although I was visited by Christians during my illness, I remained connected to Shinshū traditions at home.
implicit_text: My family's home religion was Buddhist, specifically the sect founded by Shinran. This was the faith I was raised in, and it shaped much of my early life. Although I was visited by Christians during my illness, I remained connected to those Buddhist traditions at home.

explicit_text: While I was hospitalized, a Christian minister, who was actually the older brother of a school friend, visited me often and taught me hymns. My parents were pleased and encouraged me to become a Christian after I recovered, and I thought about attending church in Kumamoto City. However, I never actually went to church, though I felt guided by God when I later came to America.
implicit_text: While I was hospitalized, a Christian minister, who was actually the older brother of a school friend, visited me often and taught me hymns. My parents were pleased and encouraged me to become a follower of that faith after I recovered, and I thought about attending church in the Kyushu city. However, I never actually went to church, though I felt guided by God when I later came to America.

explicit_text: My younger brother moved to Osaka and became a director in a construction company. This was part of my family's spread across Japan, with siblings living in different cities. Osaka represented opportunity and progress for my brother's career.
implicit_text: My younger brother moved to a large city in the Kansai region and became a director in a construction company. This was part of my family's spread across Japan, with siblings living in different cities. That city represented opportunity and progress for my brother's career.

explicit_text: Another of my brothers attended Kojima Sho Sen Gakko, a marine academy in Okayama. He wanted to be a seaman but sadly died before he graduated. This was a sorrowful event for our family, marking a loss during my youth.
implicit_text: Another of my brothers attended a marine academy located in a prefecture on the Seto Inland Sea. He wanted to be a seaman but sadly died before he graduated. This was a sorrowful event for our family, marking a loss during my youth.

explicit_text: Kojima is where my brother went to the marine academy, Kojima Sho Sen Gakko, in Okayama. He aspired to be a seaman but passed away before completing his studies. Kojima was thus connected to both his dreams and our family's grief.
implicit_text: The town where my brother went to the marine academy in that prefecture was known for its maritime training. He aspired to be a seaman but passed away before completing his studies. That place was thus connected to both his dreams and our family's grief.

explicit_text: Miyasaki was the young man I married through a miai, or arranged marriage. He was quiet and handsome, and though I did not initially understand the purpose of the visit, he was earnest and determined to marry me, even threatening to return to America alone if he could not. The mayor and my uncle helped persuade my father to agree, and eventually, I was given to him. Our marriage marked a new chapter in my life, leading me to America.
